title: syringa × henryi
intro: Syringa × henryi, also known as the Henry's lilac or Chinese lilac, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the olive family (Oleaceae) and is a cross between Syringa chinensis and Syringa oblata. The plant's flowers and leaves have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Syringa × henryi include reducing fever, relieving headaches and body aches, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Syringa × henryi can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water, or it can be made into a tincture by soaking the plant material in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
